questions.question: Explain the two different perspectives from which an Operating System can be viewed: System View and User View.
keyPoints: System View: OS as resource manager that allocates and controls hardware resources
User View: OS as interface provider giving convenient way to interact with computer
Both perspectives complement each other for overall system functioning

questions.question: Distinguish between batch processing and time-sharing systems with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Batch processing: Jobs prepared offline, executed sequentially without user interaction (e.g., early mainframe systems)
Time-sharing: Multiple users access system simultaneously through terminals with quick response (e.g., UNIX)
Time-sharing enables interactive computing unlike batch processing

questions.question: What are the five essential services provided by an Operating System to user programs?
keyPoints: Program Execution: Loading and running applications
I/O Operations: Managing input/output devices
File System Manipulation: Creating, reading, writing, deleting files
Communication: Allowing processes to exchange data
Error Detection: Detecting and handling errors in CPU, memory, or I/O devices

questions.question: Explain the boot process of a computer system.
keyPoints: Power ON triggers BIOS/UEFI initialization
BIOS performs POST (Power-On Self-Test) to check hardware
Boot device is identified and bootloader is loaded from storage
Bootloader loads OS kernel into memory
OS kernel initializes system services and starts user environment

questions.question: What is multiprogramming? How does it improve CPU utilization?
keyPoints: Multiprogramming keeps multiple programs in memory simultaneously
When one program waits for I/O, CPU switches to another ready program
This eliminates CPU idle time and increases throughput
OS scheduler decides which process gets CPU time next

questions.question: Define system calls and explain their significance in OS functioning.
keyPoints: System calls provide interface between user programs and OS kernel
They allow applications to request privileged operations
Types include: process control, file management, device management, information maintenance, communication
System calls act as gatekeepers protecting kernel from unauthorized access

questions.question: Differentiate between application software and system software with examples.
keyPoints: Application software: Programs for specific user tasks like MS Word, Chrome browser, games
System software: Programs that manage hardware and provide platform for applications like OS, device drivers, compilers
System software forms foundation on which application software runs

questions.question: Discuss the evolution of operating systems from batch systems to modern distributed systems. Explain each major phase with characteristics and examples.
keyPoints: Batch Systems: Jobs prepared on punch cards, processed sequentially, no user interaction, low CPU utilization during I/O wait
Multiprogramming: Multiple jobs in memory, CPU switches when one waits, improved utilization
Time-Sharing: Multiple users via terminals, quick response time, interactive computing emerged
Personal Computers: Microprocessors made computers affordable, single-user OS like DOS, GUI introduced
Distributed Systems: Multiple computers networked, appear as single system, resource sharing, examples: network OS, distributed databases

questions.question: Explain the role of an Operating System as a Resource Manager. Discuss how it manages CPU, memory, and I/O devices with examples.
keyPoints: CPU Management: Process scheduling algorithms decide which process gets CPU time, context switching
Memory Management: Allocates memory to processes, tracks usage, prevents unauthorized access, virtual memory
I/O Management: Uses device drivers, buffers data, handles interrupts, provides uniform interface for different devices
Fair allocation: All processes get fair share of resources
Efficiency: Optimizes resource use, minimizes wait time, maximizes throughput

questions.question: What are the main components of an Operating System? Explain the functions of kernel, shell, and command interpreter.
keyPoints: Kernel: Core component with privileged access to hardware, manages resources, provides basic OS services
Shell/Command Interpreter: Interface between user and OS, accepts commands, interprets and executes them, can be CLI or GUI
System Calls: Interface between user programs and kernel services
File System: Manages files on storage devices
Device Drivers: Enable communication between OS and hardware devices
Memory Manager: Handles RAM allocation and deallocation

questions.question: Explain the different types of operating systems: Batch, Multiprogrammed, Time-Sharing, Real-Time, Distributed, and Embedded with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Batch OS: Processes jobs in groups, no interaction (Early IBM systems)
Multiprogrammed OS: Multiple jobs in memory, CPU switching (Windows, Linux)
Time-Sharing OS: Interactive multi-user (UNIX, Linux)
Real-Time OS: Guaranteed response time for critical applications (Embedded systems in medical, aerospace)
Distributed OS: Multiple computers work as single system (Google clusters, cloud systems)
Embedded OS: Designed for specific devices with limited resources (Android, iOS in smartphones)
